HOW TO USE POWERTRADE

Getting Started

Welcome to PowerTrade! This guide will help you get started with our cryptocurrency trading platform.

What is PowerTrade?

At PowerTrade, we're a team of crypto-native professionals and tech builders passionate about shaping the future of crypto trading. Our mission is to empower institutional firms and professional traders with a powerful, intuitive platform to access the fast-moving world of crypto derivatives.

Services we offer

  1. Crypto Options Trading: Trade USD-settled, European-style options on BTC, ETH, and 80+ altcoins — more than any other platform.
  2. Perpetuals, Futures, and Spot Markets: Access deep liquidity across perpetual contracts, futures, and spot pairs for dynamic portfolio management.
  3. Request-for-Quote (RFQ) System: Execute block trades or build multi-leg structures with our Request-for-Quote interface — fast, secure, and anonymous.
  4. Multi-Leg Strategies: Execute complex trading strategies by combining multiple options contracts with different strike prices and expiration dates.
  5. One-click strategies builder: Select your bullish, rangebound or bearish strategy with these pre-built strategies.
  6. Short-term expiration, "DEGEN" options: Enjoy 10-minute and 1-hour expiration options, with no liquidation risk, for selected tokens.
  7. Desktop and Mobile Applications: Enjoy a seamless trading experience across devices with our desktop application and mobile apps for iOS and Android.
  8. API Access: Integrate your trading tools and algorithms with our platform using our API.
  9. Competitive Fee Structure: Take advantage of our competitive fees, designed to help you maximize your trading profits.

Where to find the PowerTrade app

iOS

The iOS app is live and can be found on the iOS App Store.

Android

Our Android app is live and can be found on the Google Play Store.

Web

Our web application can be accessed at https://app.power.trade/

Next Steps

  • Account Verification — Complete KYC process
  • Make a Deposit — Fund your account
  • Start Trading — Begin with options trading

PowerTrade Login Help ›
HOW TO USE POWERTRADE

PowerTrade Login Help

Secure Sign-In & 2FA Guide for accessing your PowerTrade account.

Magic Link Sign-In

PowerTrade uses a passwordless magic link system for secure sign-in. Instead of remembering a password, you'll receive a one-time login link via email each time you sign in.

  1. Open app.power.trade or the PowerTrade mobile app
  2. Enter your registered email address
  3. Tap "Sign In"
  4. Check your email for the magic link (arrives within seconds)
  5. Click the link to be logged in automatically

Important: Magic links expire after 1 hour and can only be used once. If the link has expired, simply request a new one.

Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)

For additional account security, PowerTrade supports two-factor authentication. When enabled, you'll need to provide a second verification code after clicking the magic link.

  • Security PIN: A 4-digit PIN set during account creation that's required on each device
  • Authenticator App: Use Google Authenticator, Authy, or any TOTP-compatible app for time-based codes

Troubleshooting Login Issues

  • No email received? Check your spam/junk folder and whitelist support@power.trade
  • Link expired? Request a fresh magic link — they expire after 1 hour
  • Wrong device? Magic links must be opened on the same device/browser where you requested them
  • Web app issues? Try an incognito/private browsing window or clear your browser cache
  • Still stuck? Contact support@power.trade with your registered email

Login Security Best Practices

  • Always verify the URL is app.power.trade before entering credentials
  • Never share your magic link with anyone
  • Enable 2FA for maximum account protection
  • Log out when using shared or public computers

Still have questions?

💬 Email Support: support@power.trade

💬 Telegram 24/7 Desk: https://t.me/power_trade

Fix Email Issues ›
HOW TO USE POWERTRADE

Fix Email Issues on PowerTrade

Troubleshoot email verification and notification problems.

Not Receiving Emails?

PowerTrade sends emails for login magic links, withdrawal confirmations, deposit notifications, and account alerts. If you're not receiving them, try these steps:

  1. Check your spam/junk folder — email providers sometimes filter automated messages
  2. Whitelist PowerTrade — add support@power.trade and noreply@power.trade to your email contacts or safe sender list
  3. Check your email address — verify you registered with the correct email address on PowerTrade
  4. Wait a few minutes — during high traffic, emails may be slightly delayed
  5. Try a different email provider — some corporate or institutional email servers have strict filtering rules

Email Verification Issues

During account creation, you'll receive a verification email with a magic link. Common issues include:

  • Link expired: Magic links expire after 1 hour. Request a new one from the login screen
  • Link already used: Each magic link can only be used once. Request a fresh link if needed
  • Wrong device: Open the magic link on the same device/browser where you requested it

Managing Email Notifications

PowerTrade sends the following types of email notifications:

  • Login magic links — sent each time you sign in
  • Deposit confirmations — when funds are credited to your account
  • Withdrawal confirmations — requiring your approval before processing
  • Security alerts — for new device logins or account changes

Changing Your Email Address

To change the email address associated with your PowerTrade account, contact our support team at support@power.trade. For security purposes, email changes require identity verification.

Subaccounts ›
HOW TO USE POWERTRADE

Create & Manage Subaccounts on PowerTrade

Strategy segmentation through subaccount management on PowerTrade.

What Are Subaccounts?

Subaccounts provide the ability to segregate trading activity from your main account. Since PowerTrade accounts have portfolio margin enabled by default, creating subaccounts allows you to isolate your positions and margin balances for different strategies.

Benefits of Subaccounts

  • Strategy Isolation: Run different trading strategies independently with separate margin pools
  • Risk Management: Isolate high-risk positions from your main portfolio to prevent cross-contamination
  • Performance Tracking: Track individual strategy performance clearly without mixing P&L
  • Unlimited Subaccounts: Create as many subaccounts as needed at no additional cost

Creating a Subaccount

  1. Navigate to Settings → Subaccounts in the PowerTrade app
  2. Click "Create Subaccount"
  3. Give your subaccount a descriptive name (e.g., "BTC Options", "Altcoin Perpetuals")
  4. Your subaccount is ready to use immediately

Internal Transfers

Move funds between your main account and subaccounts instantly:

  • Free transfers — no blockchain fees for internal movements
  • Instant settlement — funds are available immediately
  • Margin impact — transfers affect the account health of both accounts, so plan accordingly

Note: Withdrawals can only be initiated from your main account. Transfer funds back to your main account before withdrawing.

Security Tips ›
HOW TO USE POWERTRADE

PowerTrade Security Tips

Protect your crypto trading account with these essential security best practices.

Account Security

  • Enable 2FA: Use an authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy) for an extra layer of protection
  • Use a strong security PIN: Choose a unique 4-digit PIN that you don't use elsewhere
  • Secure your email: Your email is your primary login method — use a strong password and 2FA on your email account too
  • Verify URLs: Always check that you're on app.power.trade before signing in

Phishing Prevention

Be vigilant against phishing attempts targeting cryptocurrency traders:

  • Official domains only: PowerTrade will only contact you from @power.trade email addresses
  • Never share magic links: PowerTrade staff will never ask for your login link or security PIN
  • Beware of impersonators: Verify any communication claiming to be from PowerTrade through official channels
  • Bookmark the app: Save https://app.power.trade as a bookmark to avoid typosquatting sites

Withdrawal Security

  • Address whitelisting: New withdrawal addresses require email verification before first use
  • Double-check addresses: Always verify the full wallet address before confirming a withdrawal
  • Email confirmation: Every withdrawal requires email confirmation (link expires in 10 minutes)
  • Start small: When withdrawing to a new address, send a small test amount first

Device Security

  • Keep your operating system and browser up to date
  • Use antivirus software and keep it current
  • Avoid using public Wi-Fi for trading — use a VPN if necessary
  • Log out of PowerTrade when using shared or public devices
  • Keep your mobile app updated to the latest version

Deposits ›
HOW TO USE POWERTRADE

Deposits on PowerTrade

Learn how to deposit USDC, BTC, ETH and altcoins safely into PowerTrade. Supported assets, confirmations and tips.

Supported Assets

Users can fund their trading accounts with:

  • ERC20 USDC tokens
  • Bitcoin (BTC)
  • Ethereum (ETH)
  • PowerTrade Fuel (PTF)
  • Solana (SOL)
  • Avalanche (AVAX)
  • Binance Coin (BNB)
  • Dogecoin (DOGE)
  • Chainlink (LINK)
  • Litecoin (LTC)
  • SUI Network (SUI)
  • Toncoin (TON)
  • Ripple (XRP)

How to Deposit (Web)

  1. Go to your Funds screen by clicking on the menu dropdown in the top navigation and select "Deposit Add funds"
  2. Click on the "Deposit" button next to the asset you want to deposit
  3. Your PowerTrade wallet address will be revealed. You can also click the QR code icon to see your wallet's address in QR code form
  4. Send funds from your external wallet to this address

Important: Make sure to only transfer the correct type of asset into the wallet. Transferring the wrong type of cryptocurrency may render it unrecoverable!

How to Deposit (Mobile)

  1. Tap on the Wallet icon on your app
  2. Select the cryptocurrency you intend to use
  3. Tap on the "Deposit" button to reveal your PowerTrade wallet address
  4. You can also tap on the QR code icon to reveal your wallet's address in QR code form
  5. Send funds from your external wallet

Confirmation

Once the transaction is confirmed on the blockchain, your wallet balance will be updated. You will also receive an email confirmation of the transaction.

Troubleshooting Delayed Deposits

Common causes of delays:

  • Transaction wasn't broadcast — Check if your transaction appears on the blockchain explorer
  • Network Congestion — High traffic can delay processing
  • Low fees — Transactions with higher fees get priority
  • Prior unconfirmed transactions — Wait for previous transactions to confirm

Withdrawals ›
HOW TO USE POWERTRADE

Withdrawals from PowerTrade

How to cash out crypto safely from your PowerTrade account.

Withdrawal Process

  1. Go to the Funds screen and click "Withdraw" for your chosen asset
  2. Add and verify a withdrawal address (one-time setup — requires email confirmation)
  3. Enter the withdrawal amount
  4. Review the transaction details and confirm
  5. Confirm via the email link sent to your registered address (expires in 10 minutes)
  6. Monitor the withdrawal status in the Withdrawal tab

Address Whitelisting

For security, PowerTrade requires you to whitelist withdrawal addresses before use:

  • New addresses must be verified via email before the first withdrawal
  • Once verified, the address is saved for future withdrawals
  • You can manage your whitelisted addresses in Settings → Withdrawal Addresses

Withdrawal Limits

  • Unverified accounts: 1,000 USDC per 24 hours
  • Verified accounts: Unlimited withdrawals

Complete KYC verification to remove withdrawal limits.

Withdrawal Fees

Withdrawal fees are dynamic and based on current network conditions. The fee is displayed before you confirm the withdrawal. Fees cover the blockchain transaction (gas/miner) costs.

Processing Times

Most withdrawals are processed within minutes. Processing time depends on:

  • Network congestion — busy blockchains take longer
  • Security reviews — large withdrawals may undergo additional review
  • Email confirmation — you must click the confirmation link within 10 minutes

Account Verification ›
HOW TO USE POWERTRADE

Account Verification Guide

KYC limits, verification levels, and security requirements.

Why Verify Your Account?

Account verification (KYC — Know Your Customer) unlocks full platform access, including higher withdrawal limits, enhanced security, and faster account support.

Verification Levels

  • Unverified: 1,000 USDC per 24-hour withdrawal limit. Basic trading access.
  • Verified (KYC Complete): Unlimited withdrawals. Full platform access including all trading features.

Required Documents

To complete KYC verification, you'll need:

  • Proof of Identity (POI): Valid government-issued ID — passport (preferred), national ID card, or driver's license
  • Proof of Address (POA): Utility bill, bank statement, or government letter dated within the last 3 months
  • Selfie verification: A live photo matching your ID document

Verification Process

  1. Navigate to Settings → Verification in the PowerTrade app
  2. Upload a clear photo or scan of your identity document
  3. Upload your proof of address document
  4. Complete the selfie verification step
  5. Submit for review — verification is typically processed within 24–48 hours

Tips for Faster Approval

  • Ensure all document photos are clear, well-lit, and fully visible
  • Use a passport as your primary ID (highest acceptance rate)
  • Make sure proof of address is dated within the last 3 months
  • Your name on all documents must match your PowerTrade account name

Corporate Accounts ›
HOW TO USE POWERTRADE

Corporate Accounts on PowerTrade

Institutional trading setup and corporate account management.

Institutional Trading on PowerTrade

PowerTrade supports corporate and institutional accounts for hedge funds, proprietary trading firms, family offices, and other professional entities. Corporate accounts benefit from dedicated support, custom fee arrangements, and advanced trading features.

Corporate Account Features

  • Dedicated account manager for onboarding and ongoing support
  • Custom fee structures based on trading volume and activity
  • API access for algorithmic and automated trading strategies
  • RFQ (Request-for-Quote) system for block trades and multi-leg structures
  • Multiple subaccounts for strategy segmentation
  • Enhanced reporting for compliance and audit requirements

Setting Up a Corporate Account

  1. Contact PowerTrade at support@power.trade to express interest in a corporate account
  2. Provide corporate documentation: certificate of incorporation, articles of association, and beneficial ownership structure
  3. Submit KYC/KYB documents for all authorized signatories and beneficial owners (>25% ownership)
  4. Complete the corporate onboarding review process
  5. Receive account activation and begin trading

Required Documentation

  • Certificate of incorporation or registration
  • Memorandum and articles of association
  • Proof of registered address
  • Beneficial ownership declaration
  • Government-issued ID for all authorized signatories
  • Board resolution authorizing the account opening

Trading Fees ›
HOW TO USE POWERTRADE

PowerTrade Trading Fees

Complete fee structure for options, perpetuals, futures & spot trading.

Trading Fees (Maker / Taker)

PowerTrade uses a maker-taker fee model. Makers add liquidity to the order book; takers remove it.

  • Spot Trading: 0.10% / 0.10%
  • BTC & ETH Options: 0.075% / 0.075%
  • Altcoin Options: 0.15% / 0.15%
  • Perpetuals: 0.075% / 0.075%
  • Futures: 0.075% / 0.075%

Other Fees

  • Withdrawal Fees: Dynamic, network-based fees covering blockchain transaction costs. Displayed before confirmation.
  • Deposit Fees: Free — PowerTrade does not charge for deposits
  • Liquidation Fee (Spot): 0.75%
  • Liquidation Fee (Options): 0.2%
  • Options Settlement Fee: 0.02% – 0.04%

Volume-Based Discounts

High-volume traders and institutional accounts may qualify for custom fee structures. Contact support@power.trade to discuss volume-based pricing arrangements.

PTF Token Benefits

PowerTrade Fuel (PTF) token holders may benefit from fee discounts and other platform incentives. Check the PowerTrade app for current PTF-related promotions.

Options Trading ›
TRADING

Options Trading on PowerTrade

Learn to trade crypto options. Calls, puts, spreads & hedges for BTC, ETH and 80+ altcoin markets.

What Are Crypto Options?

Crypto options are financial derivatives that give traders the right, but not the obligation, to buy or sell an underlying cryptocurrency at a fixed price (the strike price) on or before a specific date.

Unlike spot trading or perpetual futures, options allow you to limit your downside while keeping unlimited upside potential. On PowerTrade, all options are European-style and settled in USDC.

Call Options

A call option gives the buyer the right (not the obligation) to buy the cryptocurrency at the strike price.

  • Traders buy calls when they expect the crypto price to rise
  • Profit potential is unlimited if the price moves above the strike price

Example: A trader buys a BTC call option with a $50,000 strike price. If BTC rises to $55,000 before expiry, the trader can buy at $50,000 and capture the $5,000 difference (minus the premium).

Put Options

A put option gives the buyer the right (not the obligation) to sell the cryptocurrency at the strike price.

  • Traders buy puts when they expect the crypto price to fall
  • Useful for hedging against downside risk

Example: A trader buys an ETH put option with a $3,000 strike price. If ETH drops to $2,500, the trader can sell at $3,000, profiting $500 (minus the premium).

European Style Options

PowerTrade offers European-style options, which can only be exercised at the expiration date. This structure:

  • Prevents early exercise risk
  • Simplifies strategy planning
  • Keeps the focus on price moves up to expiry

USDC Cash-Settled Options

All PowerTrade options are cash-settled in USDC:

  • No need to hold BTC, ETH, or altcoins directly
  • Settlement in stable USDC makes it easy to manage profits and losses
  • Reduces custody risk compared to physically settled options

Why Trade Options on PowerTrade?

  • ✅ 80+ altcoin options markets (the most in crypto)
  • ✅ Tight pricing & deep liquidity

‹ Trading Fees

Options Introduction ›
TRADING › OPTIONS

Options Introduction

European, USDC-Settled Crypto Options on PowerTrade.

What Are Crypto Options?

Crypto options are financial derivatives that give traders the right, but not the obligation, to buy or sell an underlying cryptocurrency at a fixed price (the strike price) on a specific date. Unlike spot trading or perpetual futures, options allow you to limit your downside while keeping unlimited upside potential.

European-Style Options

All options on PowerTrade are European-style, meaning they can only be exercised at the expiration date — not before. This structure:

  • Prevents early exercise risk for sellers
  • Simplifies strategy planning for all traders
  • Keeps focus on price movement up to expiry
  • Provides cleaner Greeks and pricing models

USDC Cash Settlement

All PowerTrade options are cash-settled in USDC. At expiration, the profit or loss is calculated and settled in USDC directly to your account:

  • No need to hold BTC, ETH, or altcoins directly
  • Settlement in stable USDC makes P&L management simple
  • Reduces custody risk compared to physically settled options
  • All collateral is denominated in USDC

Option Types

  • Call Options: Give you the right to buy at the strike price. Profit when the underlying price rises above the strike + premium paid.
  • Put Options: Give you the right to sell at the strike price. Profit when the underlying price falls below the strike – premium paid.

DEGEN Options

PowerTrade offers short-term expiration "DEGEN" options with 10-minute and 1-hour expirations. These are ideal for traders who want quick, defined-risk positions on short-term price movements — with no liquidation risk.

Still have questions?

💬 Email Support: support@power.trade

💬 Telegram 24/7 Desk: https://t.me/power_trade

‹ Options Trading

Options Contract Specifications ›
TRADING › OPTIONS

PowerTrade Options Contract Specifications

BTC, ETH & Altcoin options contract details.

General Specifications

  • Style: European (exercisable only at expiry)
  • Settlement: Cash-settled in USDC
  • Collateral: USDC
  • Contract size: 1 unit of the underlying asset
  • Tick size: 0.01 USDC

BTC Options

  • Underlying: BTC/USD index price
  • Expirations: Daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly
  • Strike intervals: $500 – $5,000 depending on expiry
  • Trading fees: Maker 0.075% / Taker 0.075%

ETH Options

  • Underlying: ETH/USD index price
  • Expirations: Daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly
  • Strike intervals: $25 – $250 depending on expiry
  • Trading fees: Maker 0.075% / Taker 0.075%

Altcoin Options (80+ Markets)

PowerTrade offers the widest altcoin options coverage in crypto, including SOL, AVAX, DOGE, LINK, SUI, TON, XRP, and 70+ more. Altcoin options share these specs:

  • Expirations: Daily to monthly
  • Settlement: USDC cash-settled
  • Trading fees: Maker 0.15% / Taker 0.15%

Expiration & Settlement

Options expire at 08:00 UTC on the expiration date. Settlement is automatic — if your option is in-the-money at expiry, the profit is credited to your USDC balance. Out-of-the-money options expire worthless with no further action required.

BTC Options Trading – Contract Specs, DEGEN Options & Strategies on PowerTrade

Bitcoin (BTC) options with expirations, strategies, and institutional RFQ access.

Bitcoin (BTC) is the world's largest cryptocurrency and the cornerstone of crypto derivatives trading. On PowerTrade, traders gain access to BTC options with flexible expirations, DEGEN ultra-short contracts, RFQ block trading, and deep strike coverage — giving both retail and institutional traders the tools to trade BTC volatility.

👉 Start trading BTC options on PowerTrade or PowerDEX.

What Are BTC Options?

BTC options are financial derivatives that give you the right but not the obligation to buy or sell Bitcoin at a chosen strike price before expiry.

  • BTC Call options → Profit from rising BTC prices.
  • BTC Put options → Hedge downside or profit from corrections.

Options give traders the ability to hedge, speculate, and trade volatility with defined downside risk — unlike perpetuals, where sudden moves can lead to liquidation.

Expiration Dates Available

PowerTrade offers BTC options with expirations ranging from same-day (0DTE) up to 12 months.

  • Short-dated BTC options (daily / weekly) → Great for momentum plays and event-driven trading.
  • Longer-dated BTC options (monthly / quarterly) → Used for portfolio hedging and structured strategies.

DEGEN Options – 10min & 1h Trading

For traders who thrive on intraday moves, PowerTrade offers BTC DEGEN options with 10-minute and 1-hour expirations.

⚡ Why DEGEN BTC Options?

  • Capture micro-moves in Bitcoin's price action.
  • Trade news-driven volatility with no liquidation risk — your downside is fixed at premium paid.
  • Perfect for event-driven trading and rapid momentum plays.

👉 Trade BTC DEGEN Options on PowerDEX.

  • BTC Protective Put → Hedge long-term holdings against market drawdowns.
  • BTC Calendar Spread → Trade time decay differences between short and long-dated options.

Technical Analysis & Market Sentiment

📊 Bitcoin dominance, ETF flows, halving cycles, and macro events (CPI, FOMC) drive BTC options volume. Institutional adoption through spot ETFs has added a new layer of demand for hedging tools.

What Are APT Options?

APT options are financial derivatives that give you the right but not the obligation to buy or sell Aptos at a chosen strike price before expiry.

  • APT Call options → Profit from rising APT prices.
  • APT Put options → Hedge downside or profit from corrections.

Options give traders the ability to hedge, speculate, and trade volatility with defined downside risk — unlike perpetuals, where sudden moves can lead to liquidation.

What Are CRV Options?

CRV options are financial derivatives that give you the right but not the obligation to buy or sell Curve at a chosen strike price before expiry.

ETC Options Trading – Contract Specs, Strategies & Market Insights on PowerTrade

Ethereum Classic (ETC) options with expirations, strategies, and institutional RFQ access.

Ethereum Classic (ETC) is the original Ethereum chain maintained after the DAO hack fork, continuing with proof-of-work consensus. On PowerTrade, traders gain access to ETC options with flexible expirations, RFQ block trading, and deep strike coverage — giving both retail and institutional traders the tools to trade ETC volatility.

👉 Start trading ETC options on PowerTrade or PowerDEX.

What Are ETC Options?

ETC options are financial derivatives that give you the right but not the obligation to buy or sell Ethereum Classic at a chosen strike price before expiry.

  • ETC Call options → Profit from rising ETC prices.
  • ETC Put options → Hedge downside or profit from corrections.

Options give traders the ability to hedge, speculate, and trade volatility with defined downside risk — unlike perpetuals, where sudden moves can lead to liquidation.

Expiration Dates Available

PowerTrade offers ETC options with expirations ranging from same-day (0DTE) up to 12 months.

  • Short-dated ETC options (daily / weekly) → Great for momentum plays and event-driven trading.
  • Longer-dated ETC options (monthly / quarterly) → Used for portfolio hedging and structured strategies.

GMT Options Trading – Contract Specs, Strategies & Market Insights on PowerTrade

GMT (GMT) options with expirations, strategies, and institutional RFQ access.

GMT (GMT) is the governance token of STEPN, the move-to-earn lifestyle app that pioneered fitness-based crypto rewards. On PowerTrade, traders gain access to GMT options with flexible expirations, RFQ block trading, and deep strike coverage — giving both retail and institutional traders the tools to trade GMT volatility.

👉 Start trading GMT options on PowerTrade or PowerDEX.

What Are GMT Options?

GMT options are financial derivatives that give you the right but not the obligation to buy or sell GMT at a chosen strike price before expiry.

  • GMT Call options → Profit from rising GMT prices.
  • GMT Put options → Hedge downside or profit from corrections.

Options give traders the ability to hedge, speculate, and trade volatility with defined downside risk — unlike perpetuals, where sudden moves can lead to liquidation.

Expiration Dates Available

PowerTrade offers GMT options with expirations ranging from same-day (0DTE) up to 12 months.

  • Short-dated GMT options (daily / weekly) → Great for momentum plays and event-driven trading.
  • Longer-dated GMT options (monthly / quarterly) → Used for portfolio hedging and structured strategies.

Contract Specifications

UnderlyingGMT (GMT)
Ticker$GMT
# Expirations8 (0DTE → 12 months)
Settlement IndexGMT CoinGecko Index
Quote CurrencyUSDC
Min Trade Size1 GMT Option Contract
Premium Decimals5
Collateral Accepted❌ No
Order BookPowerTrade GMT Options
DEX LinkPowerDEX GMT Options
Supported RFQ StrategiesGMT Calls, GMT Puts, GMT Call Spreads, GMT Put Spreads, GMT 1x2 Call Spreads, GMT 1x3 Put Spreads, GMT Call Butterfly, GMT Put Butterfly, GMT Iron Butterfly, GMT Call Condor, GMT Put Condor, GMT Iron Condor, GMT Straddle, GMT Strangle, GMT Risk Reversal, GMT Call Calendar Spread, GMT Put Calendar Spread, GMT Seagull, GMT Box Spread
Min RFQ Size1 GMT Option Contract

RFQ & Liquidity Access

Institutions can trade GMT options through PowerTrade's RFQ (Request for Quote) system, which provides block-trade execution, tight bid-offer spreads, and deep liquidity. Retail traders can access the order book for instant fills.

👉 Join our 24/7 RFQ desk on Telegram.

Why Trade GMT Options on PowerTrade?

GMT operates in the Web3 gaming and entertainment space — options allow traders to take positions on sector growth without forced liquidation risk.

  • ✅ Expirations from 0DTE to 12 months
  • ✅ RFQ block trades for institutions
  • ✅ Retail-friendly order book execution
  • ✅ No liquidation risk vs perpetuals
  • ✅ 80+ altcoin option markets — largest in crypto
  • ✅ Trade on both CEX (PowerTrade) & DEX (PowerDEX)

Example GMT Option Strategies

  • GMT Call Spread → Position for gaming/metaverse sector growth with defined risk.
  • GMT Protective Put → Hedge volatile gaming token exposure.
  • GMT Strangle → Profit from large moves around game launches or partnership announcements.

Technical Analysis & Market Sentiment

📊 GMT options flow tracks move-to-earn adoption metrics, STEPN ecosystem updates, and broader GameFi/lifestyle app sentiment.

How to Get Started

  1. Create an account on PowerTrade or PowerDEX.
  2. Deposit USDC.
  3. Navigate to the GMT options page.
  4. Select expirations and strikes.
  5. Execute trades via order book or RFQ.

What Are INJ Options?

INJ options are financial derivatives that give you the right but not the obligation to buy or sell Injective at a chosen strike price before expiry.

  • INJ Call options → Profit from rising INJ prices.
  • INJ Put options → Hedge downside or profit from corrections.

Options give traders the ability to hedge, speculate, and trade volatility with defined downside risk — unlike perpetuals, where sudden moves can lead to liquidation.

PENDLE Options Trading – Contract Specs, Strategies & Market Insights on PowerTrade

Pendle (PENDLE) options with expirations, strategies, and institutional RFQ access.

Pendle (PENDLE) is a DeFi yield trading protocol that tokenizes and trades future yield, enabling fixed-rate strategies and yield speculation. On PowerTrade, traders gain access to PENDLE options with flexible expirations, RFQ block trading, and deep strike coverage — giving both retail and institutional traders the tools to trade PENDLE volatility.

👉 Start trading PENDLE options on PowerTrade or PowerDEX.

What Are PENDLE Options?

PENDLE options are financial derivatives that give you the right but not the obligation to buy or sell Pendle at a chosen strike price before expiry.

  • PENDLE Call options → Profit from rising PENDLE prices.
  • PENDLE Put options → Hedge downside or profit from corrections.

Options give traders the ability to hedge, speculate, and trade volatility with defined downside risk — unlike perpetuals, where sudden moves can lead to liquidation.

Expiration Dates Available

PowerTrade offers PENDLE options with expirations ranging from same-day (0DTE) up to 12 months.

  • Short-dated PENDLE options (daily / weekly) → Great for momentum plays and event-driven trading.
  • Longer-dated PENDLE options (monthly / quarterly) → Used for portfolio hedging and structured strategies.

TRB Options Trading – Contract Specs, Strategies & Market Insights on PowerTrade

Tellor (TRB) options with expirations, strategies, and institutional RFQ access.

Tellor (TRB) is a decentralized oracle protocol providing tamper-resistant data for smart contracts through a network of reporters. On PowerTrade, traders gain access to TRB options with flexible expirations, RFQ block trading, and deep strike coverage — giving both retail and institutional traders the tools to trade TRB volatility.

👉 Start trading TRB options on PowerTrade or PowerDEX.

What Are TRB Options?

TRB options are financial derivatives that give you the right but not the obligation to buy or sell Tellor at a chosen strike price before expiry.

  • TRB Call options → Profit from rising TRB prices.
  • TRB Put options → Hedge downside or profit from corrections.

Options give traders the ability to hedge, speculate, and trade volatility with defined downside risk — unlike perpetuals, where sudden moves can lead to liquidation.

Expiration Dates Available

PowerTrade offers TRB options with expirations ranging from same-day (0DTE) up to 12 months.

  • Short-dated TRB options (daily / weekly) → Great for momentum plays and event-driven trading.
  • Longer-dated TRB options (monthly / quarterly) → Used for portfolio hedging and structured strategies.

Perpetuals Trading ›
TRADING

Perpetuals Trading on PowerTrade

Trade perpetual contracts with no expiration date and deep liquidity.

What Are Perpetual Contracts?

Perpetual contracts (perps) are derivative contracts similar to futures but with no expiration date. They allow traders to speculate on the price of cryptocurrencies with leverage, without the need to roll over contracts at expiry.

Key Features

  • No expiration: Hold positions indefinitely without worrying about contract rollovers
  • Funding rate: Periodic payments between longs and shorts to keep the contract price anchored to the spot price
  • Leverage: Trade with leverage to amplify your exposure (and risk)
  • USDC-margined: All margin and settlement in USDC for simplicity

Funding Rate

The funding rate is exchanged between long and short position holders every 8 hours. When the rate is positive, longs pay shorts. When negative, shorts pay longs. This mechanism ensures the perpetual price stays close to the underlying spot price.

How to Trade Perpetuals

  1. Navigate to the Perpetuals section in the PowerTrade app
  2. Select the trading pair (e.g., BTC-PERP, ETH-PERP)
  3. Choose your position size and leverage
  4. Place a market or limit order
  5. Monitor your position and manage risk with stop-loss and take-profit orders

Still have questions?

💬 Email Support: support@power.trade

💬 Telegram 24/7 Desk: https://t.me/power_trade

Previous
‹ Options Specifications
Next
Futures Trading ›
TRADING

Futures Trading on PowerTrade

Trade crypto futures with fixed expiration dates, deep liquidity, and competitive fees.

What Are Crypto Futures?

Futures contracts are agreements to buy or sell a cryptocurrency at a predetermined price on a specific future date. Unlike perpetual contracts, futures have a fixed expiration date, making them ideal for precise timing strategies and hedging.

Key Features

  • Fixed expiration: Contracts settle on a specific date, providing certainty for hedging strategies
  • USDC-settled: All futures on PowerTrade settle in USDC
  • Leverage available: Trade with leverage to amplify exposure
  • Multiple expirations: Weekly, monthly, and quarterly expiration cycles

Futures vs. Perpetuals

  • Expiration: Futures expire on a set date; perpetuals never expire
  • Funding rate: Perpetuals have recurring funding payments; futures do not
  • Basis: Futures may trade at a premium or discount to spot (basis); perpetuals track spot via funding
  • Use case: Futures are better for calendar strategies and hedging specific time horizons

Contract Specifications

  • Settlement: USDC cash-settled at expiry
  • Trading fees: Maker 0.075% / Taker 0.075%
  • Margin: USDC collateral with portfolio margin

Still have questions?

💬 Email Support: support@power.trade

💬 Telegram 24/7 Desk: https://t.me/power_trade

‹ Perpetuals

Spot Trading ›
TRADING

Spot Trading on PowerTrade

Buy and sell crypto directly with immediate settlement.

What Is Spot Trading?

Spot trading is the straightforward buying and selling of cryptocurrencies at the current market price with immediate settlement. When you buy BTC on the spot market, you own actual BTC that's credited to your PowerTrade wallet.

Available Trading Pairs

PowerTrade offers spot trading across major cryptocurrency pairs, all quoted against USDC:

  • BTC/USDC
  • ETH/USDC
  • SOL/USDC
  • And more — check the app for the full list of supported pairs

Order Types

  • Market orders: Execute immediately at the best available price
  • Limit orders: Set your desired price — the order fills when the market reaches it

Spot Trading Fees

Spot trading fees on PowerTrade are 0.10% for both makers and takers. See the fees page for full details.

Still have questions?

💬 Telegram 24/7 Desk: https://t.me/power_trade

Previous
‹ Futures
Next
Margin & Liquidation ›
TRADING

Margin & Liquidation

Understanding margin requirements and liquidation on PowerTrade.

Portfolio Margin

PowerTrade uses a portfolio margin system by default, which evaluates the risk of your entire portfolio holistically rather than on a per-position basis. This means hedged positions require less margin than isolated margin systems.

Margin Requirements

  • Initial margin: The minimum collateral required to open a new position
  • Maintenance margin: The minimum collateral required to keep a position open
  • Account health: Displayed as a percentage — when it drops to 0%, liquidation begins

Liquidation Process

When your account health reaches critical levels:

  1. Warning: You'll receive alerts when your account health is declining
  2. Partial liquidation: PowerTrade may reduce your positions incrementally to restore margin
  3. Full liquidation: If account health reaches 0%, remaining positions are closed at market price

Liquidation Fees

  • Spot positions: 0.75% liquidation fee
  • Options positions: 0.2% liquidation fee

Avoiding Liquidation

  • Monitor your account health regularly
  • Maintain adequate collateral above the maintenance margin
  • Use stop-loss orders to limit position losses
  • Use subaccounts to isolate high-risk strategies
  • Deposit additional USDC before your account health drops too low

Still have questions?

💬 Email Support: support@power.trade

💬 Telegram 24/7 Desk: https://t.me/power_trade

Previous
‹ Spot Trading
Next
xStocks ›
TRADING

xStocks - Tokenized Stocks

Trade tokenized stocks on the blockchain through PowerTrade.

What Are xStocks?

xStocks are tokenized representations of traditional stocks that can be traded on the blockchain through PowerTrade. They allow crypto traders to gain exposure to traditional equity markets (like AAPL, TSLA, AMZN) without leaving the crypto ecosystem.

How xStocks Work

  • Each xStock token represents exposure to the price of the underlying stock
  • Tokens are backed and track the real-time price of the underlying equity
  • Trade during extended hours — not limited to traditional stock market hours
  • All settlement and collateral in USDC

Benefits of xStocks

  • Fractional ownership: Trade any amount — no need to buy whole shares
  • Crypto-native: Fund with USDC and trade alongside your crypto portfolio
  • 24/7 availability: Extended trading hours compared to traditional markets
  • No traditional brokerage needed: Access stocks directly through your PowerTrade account

Available xStocks

PowerTrade offers a growing selection of popular US equities as xStocks. Check the PowerTrade app for the current list of available tokenized stocks.

API Overview ›
API

PowerTrade API Overview

Automate your crypto options trading with the PowerTrade API. Access markets, place orders, and manage positions programmatically.

Generate an API Key and Private Key

To start using the PowerTrade API, you'll need to generate an API key pair from the PowerTrade application:

  1. Log in to PowerTrade
  2. Navigate to Settings → API Keys
  3. Click "Create New API Key"
  4. Save your api_key and private_key securely — the private key is only shown once

Important: Store your private_key securely. It cannot be retrieved after creation.

Generate an Access Token — JWT

The PowerTrade API uses JWT (JSON Web Tokens) signed with ES256 for authentication. Here's how to generate one:

import jwt
import time
import uuid

api_key = "your_api_key"
private_key = "your_private_key"

payload = {
    "sub": api_key,
    "iat": int(time.time()),
    "exp": int(time.time()) + 3600,
    "jti": str(uuid.uuid4())
}

jwt_token = jwt.encode(
    payload,
    private_key,
    algorithm="ES256"
)

print(f"Token: {jwt_token}")

API Endpoints

The PowerTrade API provides the following base endpoints:

  • REST API: https://api.power.trade/v1/
  • WebSocket: wss://api.power.trade/v1/ws

Authentication Header

Include the JWT token in your request headers:

Authorization: Bearer <your_jwt_token>
Content-Type: application/json

API Authentication ›
API

API Authentication

Secure access to the PowerTrade Trading API using JWT tokens.

Authentication Overview

The PowerTrade API uses JWT (JSON Web Tokens) signed with the ES256 algorithm for authentication. Every authenticated API request must include a valid JWT token in the Authorization header.

API Key Management

  1. Log in to PowerTrade
  2. Navigate to Profile → API Keys
  3. Click "Create New API Key"
  4. Save your api_key and private_key securely

⚠️ Important: The private key is only displayed once at creation time. Store it securely — it cannot be retrieved later. If lost, delete the key and create a new one.

Generating a JWT Token

Use the ES256 algorithm to sign your JWT with the following payload structure:

import jwt, time, uuid

payload = {
    "sub": "your_api_key",
    "iat": int(time.time()),
    "exp": int(time.time()) + 3600,
    "jti": str(uuid.uuid4()),
    "client": "api"
}

token = jwt.encode(payload, private_key, algorithm="ES256")

Using the Token

Include the JWT token in every API request:

Authorization: Bearer <your_jwt_token>
Content-Type: application/json

Security Best Practices

  • Never expose your private key in client-side code or public repositories
  • Rotate API keys periodically
  • Set short token expiration times (1 hour recommended)
  • Use environment variables or secure vaults to store keys
  • Monitor API key usage and disable unused keys

Legal & Privacy ›
LEGAL

Legal & Privacy Policies

Terms, conditions, and privacy policies for PowerTrade.

Terms of Service

By using PowerTrade, you agree to our Terms of Service which govern your access to and use of the platform, including trading, deposits, withdrawals, and all associated services. The complete Terms of Service are available within the PowerTrade application and on our website.

Privacy Policy

PowerTrade is committed to protecting your personal data. Our Privacy Policy outlines:

  • Data collection: What personal information we collect during registration, verification, and trading
  • Data usage: How we use your information to provide and improve our services
  • Data sharing: When and with whom we may share your information (e.g., regulatory requirements, KYC providers)
  • Data security: Measures we take to protect your personal data
  • Your rights: How to access, update, or request deletion of your personal data

Cookie Policy

PowerTrade uses cookies and similar technologies to improve your experience, analyze usage patterns, and provide personalized features. You can manage cookie preferences in your browser settings.

Regulatory Compliance

PowerTrade complies with applicable laws and regulations in our operating jurisdictions. We implement AML (Anti-Money Laundering) and KYC (Know Your Customer) procedures to prevent illicit activity and protect our users.

Country Availability ›
LEGAL

Country Availability

Supported jurisdictions and country restrictions for PowerTrade.

Global Availability

PowerTrade is available in most countries worldwide. Our platform serves traders across Asia, Europe, South America, Africa, and other regions. However, certain jurisdictions are restricted due to regulatory requirements.

Restricted Jurisdictions

PowerTrade services are not available in the following jurisdictions (this list may be updated from time to time):

  • United States of America (and its territories)
  • Sanctioned countries as designated by OFAC, UN, and EU sanctions lists
  • Other jurisdictions where cryptocurrency derivatives trading is prohibited by local law

Verifying Your Eligibility

Users are responsible for ensuring that using PowerTrade is legal in their jurisdiction. During the KYC verification process, your country of residence will be verified against our supported jurisdictions list.

VPN Usage

Using a VPN to access PowerTrade from a restricted jurisdiction is a violation of our Terms of Service. Accounts found to be circumventing geographic restrictions may be suspended.

Risk Disclosure ›
LEGAL

Risk Disclosure

Important information about trading risks and warnings.

General Risk Warning

Cryptocurrency trading involves substantial risk of loss and is not suitable for all investors. The value of cryptocurrencies can fluctuate significantly, and you may lose some or all of your invested capital. Only trade with funds you can afford to lose.

Derivatives Trading Risks

  • Options risk: Options can expire worthless, resulting in the complete loss of the premium paid
  • Leverage risk: Leveraged products (perpetuals, futures) amplify both potential gains and losses
  • Liquidation risk: Leveraged positions may be liquidated if margin requirements are not maintained
  • Volatility risk: Crypto markets are highly volatile — prices can move dramatically in short periods

Market Risks

  • Liquidity risk: Some markets may have limited liquidity, resulting in wider spreads or difficulty closing positions
  • Technology risk: System failures, network issues, or smart contract bugs could affect trading
  • Regulatory risk: Changes in regulations could affect the availability or legality of certain products or services
  • Counterparty risk: Risk that the exchange or settlement infrastructure could face operational issues

Past Performance

Past performance does not guarantee future results. Historical returns, expected returns, or probability projections are not guaranteed and may not reflect actual future performance.

Not Financial Advice

Nothing on PowerTrade constitutes financial, investment, legal, or tax advice. You should consult with appropriate professionals before making any trading or investment decisions. PowerTrade does not provide personalized investment recommendations.

Security ›
LEGAL

Security

Platform security measures and how PowerTrade protects your assets.

Infrastructure Security

  • Industry-standard encryption: All data in transit and at rest is encrypted using TLS 1.3 and AES-256
  • DDoS protection: Multi-layer DDoS mitigation protects the platform from attack
  • Regular security audits: Independent penetration testing and security assessments
  • Redundant infrastructure: Multiple data centers with failover capability for high availability

Fund Security

  • Cold storage: The majority of user funds are stored in cold wallets, offline and inaccessible to potential attackers
  • Segregated accounts: User funds are segregated from operational funds
  • Multi-signature wallets: Withdrawals from hot wallets require multiple authorized signers
  • Real-time monitoring: Automated systems monitor for suspicious activity 24/7

Account Security Features

  • Passwordless authentication: Magic link login eliminates password-related vulnerabilities
  • Two-factor authentication (2FA): Additional security layer via authenticator apps
  • Withdrawal address whitelisting: New withdrawal addresses require email verification
  • Email confirmations: Critical actions (withdrawals, API key creation) require email approval
  • Session management: Automatic timeout for inactive sessions

Reporting Security Issues

If you discover a security vulnerability or suspect unauthorized access to your account, contact us immediately at support@power.trade. We take all security reports seriously and will investigate promptly.
